For an audit where a miss ships a broken service, I'll compute the answer precisely with the Go type checker rather than trust a heuristic list.

def area: .pos | split("/") | (if (.[0]=="lib" and (length>3)) then .[0:3]|join("/") elif length>2 then .[0:2]|join("/") else .[0] end);
def bucket:
  if (.pos|test("^integrations/")) then "integrations (separate module — gRPC client carriers)"
  elif (.pos|test("testenv|/backend/test/|authtest|backendtest")) then "test-support (non-_test.go helpers)"
  else area end;
["# Cluster-state storage retention audit","","Every production struct field retaining lib/backend.Backend, lib/cache.Cache, or a narrowed interface view of either.","Generated by go/types whole-program analysis (Tests:false, _test.go and testdata excluded).","","Kinds: backend-direct | backend-impl | backend-narrowed | cache-direct | cache-narrowed | services-narrowed",""] | .[],
(group_by(bucket) | sort_by(-length) | .[] |
"\n## \(.[0]|bucket) — \(length) holder(s)\n",
(.[] | "- `\(.pos)` — **\(.struct).\(.field)** `\(.type)` [\(.kind)]" + (if .carrier!=null and .carrier!="" then " via carrier `\(.carrier|split("/")|.[-1])`" else "" end) + (if .iface_decl then " — iface `\(.iface_decl|split("/")|.[-1])` {\(.methods|join(", "))}" else "" end)))
' audit5.json > /tmp/storage-retention-audit.md

cd /tmp && jq -r 'group_by(if (.pos|test("^integrations/")) then "integrations" elif (.pos|test("testenv|/backend/test/|authtest|backendtest")) then "test-support" else (.pos|split("/")|(if (.[0]=="lib" and (length>3)) then .[0:3]|join("/") elif length>2 then .[0:2]|join("/") else .[0] end)) end) | sort_by(-length) | .[] | "\(length)\t\(.[0].pos|split("/")|.[0:3]|join("/"))"' audit5.json | head -45

cd /tmp && jq -r '
def area: (.pos|split("/")) as $p | (if ($p[0]=="lib" and ($p|length)>3) then $p[0:3]|join("/") elif ($p|length)>2 then $p[0:2]|join("/") else $p[0] end);
def bucket:

  elif (.pos|test("testenv|/backend/test/|authtest|backendtest")) then "ZY test-support helpers"
  else area end;
def how: if .kind=="backend-direct" then "holds backend.Backend"
  elif .kind=="backend-impl" then "holds a concrete Backend impl"
  elif .kind=="backend-narrowed" then "narrow iface over Backend"
  elif .kind=="cache-direct" then "holds *cache.Cache"
  elif .kind=="cache-narrowed" then "narrow iface the cache satisfies"

group_by(bucket) | sort_by(-length) | .[] |
"\n@@ \(.[0]|bucket) (\(length))",
(.[] | "\(.pos) | \(.struct).\(.field) | \(.type) | \(how)" + (if (.carrier//"")!="" then " (\(.carrier|split(".")|.[-1]))" else "" end))
' audit5.json

content.text:
Audit complete. One path note: the 4 `aggregating/…` entries are `lib/usagereporter/teleport/aggregating/…` (my path shortener clipped at the last `/teleport/`).

## Method

The storage layer is `backend.Backend` (`lib/backend/backend.go:48`); the cache over it is `cache.Cache` (`lib/cache/cache.go:532`). Sense's blast gave me ~455 interface-typed candidates, but flagged them unverified — and they contained real false positives (`via: UploadHandler`, `Authorizer`, `FS`) and missed real holders whose interface is satisfied only by a `lib/services/local` service. So I computed the answer with a whole-program `go/types` analysis (`_test.go` and `testdata` excluded) that classifies every struct field as:

| tag | meaning |
|---|---|
| **B** | field typed `backend.Backend` |
| **Bi** | field typed a concrete `Backend` implementation |
| **Bn** | narrow interface that `Backend` satisfies |
| **C** | field typed `*cache.Cache` |
| **Cn** | narrow interface that `*cache.Cache` satisfies — *the easy-to-miss kind* |
| **Sn** | narrow interface satisfied only by a `lib/services/local` service, which itself holds the backend |

I dropped 24 incidental `io.Closer` matches (`Backend` has `Close()`, so every closer field matched) and all dependency-module hits. **435 production holders remain.** Full per-holder detail including each interface's method set: `/tmp/storage-retention-audit.md`.

## The holders

## What this means for the rework

Three chokepoints dominate: `lib/auth/init.go` InitConfig (25 narrowed fields), `lib/cache/cache.go` Config (29), and `lib/auth/accesspoint/accesspoint.go` Config (28) — these three are where the storage is *distributed* to everyone else, so contract changes land there first. The genuinely fragile class is the 188 **[Cn]** holders: locally-declared one-to-six-method interfaces (`Cache`, `Reader`, `AccessPoint`, `eventsWatcher`) in files that never import `lib/cache`. Handle-lifetime changes are invisible to them at compile time — nothing forces them to observe a revoked or restarted cache.

Two caveats on completeness: the analysis is static, so a handle stashed in an `any`, a closure, or a `map[string]any` is not covered; and tier-2 (**Sn**) is deliberately scoped to interfaces satisfied by `lib/services/local` services — I excluded `auth.Server`/`TeleportProcess` as carriers because they satisfy almost every interface in the tree and produced 1341 hits, which would have been noise rather than an audit.

result:
Audit complete. One path note: the 4 `aggregating/…` entries are `lib/usagereporter/teleport/aggregating/…` (my path shortener clipped at the last `/teleport/`).

## Method

The storage layer is `backend.Backend` (`lib/backend/backend.go:48`); the cache over it is `cache.Cache` (`lib/cache/cache.go:532`). Sense's blast gave me ~455 interface-typed candidates, but flagged them unverified — and they contained real false positives (`via: UploadHandler`, `Authorizer`, `FS`) and missed real holders whose interface is satisfied only by a `lib/services/local` service. So I computed the answer with a whole-program `go/types` analysis (`_test.go` and `testdata` excluded) that classifies every struct field as:

| tag | meaning |
|---|---|
| **B** | field typed `backend.Backend` |
| **Bi** | field typed a concrete `Backend` implementation |
| **Bn** | narrow interface that `Backend` satisfies |
| **C** | field typed `*cache.Cache` |
| **Cn** | narrow interface that `*cache.Cache` satisfies — *the easy-to-miss kind* |
| **Sn** | narrow interface satisfied only by a `lib/services/local` service, which itself holds the backend |

I dropped 24 incidental `io.Closer` matches (`Backend` has `Close()`, so every closer field matched) and all dependency-module hits. **435 production holders remain.** Full per-holder detail including each interface's method set: `/tmp/storage-retention-audit.md`.

## What this means for the rework

Three chokepoints dominate: `lib/auth/init.go` InitConfig (25 narrowed fields), `lib/cache/cache.go` Config (29), and `lib/auth/accesspoint/accesspoint.go` Config (28) — these three are where the storage is *distributed* to everyone else, so contract changes land there first. The genuinely fragile class is the 188 **[Cn]** holders: locally-declared one-to-six-method interfaces (`Cache`, `Reader`, `AccessPoint`, `eventsWatcher`) in files that never import `lib/cache`. Handle-lifetime changes are invisible to them at compile time — nothing forces them to observe a revoked or restarted cache.

Two caveats on completeness: the analysis is static, so a handle stashed in an `any`, a closure, or a `map[string]any` is not covered; and tier-2 (**Sn**) is deliberately scoped to interfaces satisfied by `lib/services/local` services — I excluded `auth.Server`/`TeleportProcess` as carriers because they satisfy almost every interface in the tree and produced 1341 hits, which would have been noise rather than an audit.
